purpose: Explicit list of user intent patterns that cannot be answered from DGSTREAM.VW_DEAL_ORDER_SUMMARY. When the LLM detects any of these patterns it MUST respond with the user_message immediately — do NOT attempt entity_search, do NOT attempt SQL generation.
policy: Match patterns case-insensitively and as substrings/phrases. If the ask partially overlaps with a supported ask, answer the supported part and surface the unsupported part as a separate note. IMPORTANT — compound asks: scan the ENTIRE ask and detect ALL matching unsupported intents.

unsupported_intents.reason: No peer company relationship graph or peer mapping table is available in this domain. Peer relationships require an external entity graph that is not part of VW_DEAL_ORDER_SUMMARY.
unsupported_intents.user_message: I'm unable to answer questions about peer companies — this domain does not have a peer company relationship graph. I can search for investors in a specific sector or named company instead. Would you like that?

unsupported_intents.reason: VW_DEAL_ORDER_SUMMARY has SETTLEMENT_TS (date) but no SETTLEMENT_CURRENCY column. Settlement currency data lives in the tradebook, which is a separate source.
unsupported_intents.user_message: I can't filter by settlement currency — the settlement currency field is not available in this dataset (it comes from the tradebook). I can filter by deal pricing currency (CURRENCY column) instead. Would that work?

unsupported_intents.reason: VW_DEAL_ORDER_SUMMARY does not have issuer country of domicile or country of incorporation columns. Issuer geography is not stored in this view.
unsupported_intents.user_message: I'm unable to filter by issuer country of domicile or incorporation — those fields are not available in the deal orderbook view. I can filter by deal region (TRANCHE_REGION: NAM, EMEA, APAC) as a geographic proxy. Would that work?

unsupported_intents.reason: No order match/matched status column exists in VW_DEAL_ORDER_SUMMARY.
unsupported_intents.user_message: Matched order status is not available in this dataset. I can filter orders by deal status (Priced/Settled/Closed) or investor category instead.

unsupported_intents.reason: VW_DEAL_ORDER_SUMMARY does not have an investor region or investor country column. Investor geography is not stored in this view. Do NOT substitute TRANCHE_REGION/DEAL_REGION for investor-region intent — those are the deal/tranche geography, not the investor's.
unsupported_intents.user_message: I'm unable to filter by investor region or investor country — those fields are not in this dataset (TRANCHE_REGION is the deal/tranche region, not the investor's region). I can filter by investor category (Long Only, Hedge Fund, etc.) or by deal/tranche region instead. Would that work?

unsupported_intents.reason: No TTW (Take the Wall) flag column exists in VW_DEAL_ORDER_SUMMARY.
unsupported_intents.user_message: TTW (Take the Wall) order information is not available in this dataset — the TTW flag is not exposed in the deal orderbook view. Please raise this with the product team if it is needed.

unsupported_intents.reason: VW_DEAL_ORDER_SUMMARY does not have a column attributing allocation to a specific syndicate bank. ALLOCATION is an investor-side metric; per-bank allocation attribution is not in this view.
unsupported_intents.user_message: Per-bank allocation attribution is not available in this dataset — I can't tell you how much each syndicate bank allocated. I can show you total allocation per investor for a deal, or show you which banks were in the syndicate.

unsupported_intents.reason: VW_DEAL_ORDER_SUMMARY has DEAL_STATUS which includes 'cancelled'/'deleted' at the DEAL level, not at the individual ORDER level. Per-order cancellation/deletion status is not in this view.
unsupported_intents.user_message: Order-level cancellation/deletion status is not available in this view. I can filter deals by deal status (cancelled/deleted) if you meant deal-level status. Would that work?

partial_support.supported: Filter DEAL_STATUS IN ('cancelled','deleted') for deal-level cancelled/deleted deals
